result.stdout:
Description:

Usage:
  CSharpier [command] [options]

Options:
  --version       Show version information
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

Commands:
  format <directoryOrFile>  Format files.
  check <directoryOrFile>   Check that files are formatted. Will not write any changes.
  pipe-files                Keep csharpier running so that multiples files can be piped to it via stdin.
  server                    Run CSharpier as a server so that multiple files may be formatted.

commands.payload:
Description:

Usage:
  CSharpier [command] [options]

Options:
  --version       Show version information
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

Commands:
  format <directoryOrFile>  Format files.
  check <directoryOrFile>   Check that files are formatted. Will not write any changes.
  pipe-files                Keep csharpier running so that multiples files can be piped to it via stdin.
  server                    Run CSharpier as a server so that multiple files may be formatted.

result.stdout:
Description:
  Check that files are formatted. Will not write any changes.

Usage:
  CSharpier check [<directoryOrFile>...] [options]

Arguments:
  <directoryOrFile>  One or more paths to - a directory containing files to format or a specific file to format. May be omitted when piping data via stdin.

Options:
  --config-path <config-path>                                        Path to the CSharpier configuration file
  --ignore-path <ignore-path>                                        Path to the CSharpier ignore file
  --log-format <Console|MsBuild>                                     Log output format
                                                                       Console (default) - Formats messages in a human readable way for console interaction.
                                                                       MsBuild - Formats messages in standard error/warning format for MSBuild. [default: Console]
  --log-level <Critical|Debug|Error|Information|None|Trace|Warning>  Specify the log level - Debug, Information (default), Warning, Error, None [default: Information]
  --include-generated                                                Include files generated by the SDK and files that begin with <autogenerated /> comments
  --no-msbuild-check                                                 Bypass the check to determine if a csproj files references a different version of CSharpier.MsBuild.
  --compilation-errors-as-warnings                                   Treat compilation errors from files as warnings instead of errors.
  --unformatted-as-warnings                                          Treat unformatted files as warnings instead of errors.
  -?, -h, --help                                                     Show help and usage information

  --no-msbuild-check                                                 Bypass the check to determine if a csproj files references a different version of CSharpier.MsBuild.
  --compilation-errors-as-warnings                                   Treat compilation errors from files as warnings instead of errors.

result.stdout:
Description:
  Format files.

Usage:
  CSharpier format [<directoryOrFile>...] [options]

Arguments:
  <directoryOrFile>  One or more paths to - a directory containing files to format or a specific file to format. May be omitted when piping data via stdin.

Options:
  --no-cache                                                         Bypass the cache to determine if a file needs to be formatted.
  --no-msbuild-check                                                 Bypass the check to determine if a csproj files references a different version of CSharpier.MsBuild.
  --include-generated                                                Include files generated by the SDK and files that begin with <autogenerated /> comments
  --skip-validation                                                  Skip validation of formatted files to improve performance.
  --skip-write                                                       Skip writing changes. Generally used for testing to ensure csharpier doesn't throw any errors or cause syntax tree validation failures.
  --write-stdout                                                     Write the results of formatting any files to stdout.
  --compilation-errors-as-warnings                                   Treat compilation errors from files as warnings instead of errors.
  --config-path <config-path>                                        Path to the CSharpier configuration file
  --ignore-path <ignore-path>                                        Path to the CSharpier ignore file
  --stdin-path <stdin-path>                                          Supply a path when piping input to format via stdin
  --log-format <Console|MsBuild>                                     Log output format
                                                                       Console (default) - Formats messages in a human readable way for console interaction.
                                                                       MsBuild - Formats messages in standard error/warning format for MSBuild. [default: Console]
  --log-level <Critical|Debug|Error|Information|None|Trace|Warning>  Specify the log level - Debug, Information (default), Warning, Error, None [default: Information]
  -?, -h, --help                                                     Show help and usage information

  --no-msbuild-check                                                 Bypass the check to determine if a csproj files references a different version of CSharpier.MsBuild.
  --include-generated                                                Include files generated by the SDK and files that begin with <autogenerated /> comments
  --skip-validation                                                  Skip validation of formatted files to improve performance.
  --skip-write                                                       Skip writing changes. Generally used for testing to ensure csharpier doesn't throw any errors or cause syntax tree validation failures.
  --write-stdout                                                     Write the results of formatting any files to stdout.
  --compilation-errors-as-warnings                                   Treat compilation errors from files as warnings instead of errors.
  --config-path <config-path>                                        Path to the CSharpier configuration file
  --ignore-path <ignore-path>                                        Path to the CSharpier ignore file
  --stdin-path <stdin-path>                                          Supply a path when piping input to format via stdin
  --log-format <Console|MsBuild>                                     Log output format
                                                                       Console (default) - Formats messages in a human readable way for console interaction.
                                                                       MsBuild - Formats messages in standard error/warning format for MSBuild. [default: Console]
  --log-level <Critical|Debug|Error|Information|None|Trace|Warning>  Specify the log level - Debug, Information (default), Warning, Error, None [default: Information]
  -?, -h, --help                                                     Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Run CSharpier as a server so that multiple files may be formatted.

Usage:
  CSharpier server [options]

Options:
  --server-port <server-port>  Specify the port that CSharpier should start on. Defaults to a random unused port.
  -?, -h, --help               Show help and usage information

commands.payload:
Description:
  Run CSharpier as a server so that multiple files may be formatted.

Usage:
  CSharpier server [options]

Options:
  --server-port <server-port>  Specify the port that CSharpier should start on. Defaults to a random unused port.
  -?, -h, --help               Show help and usage information
